prompts.text: You spot someone you know at New Road and you're not in the mood to talk. What's your move?
How many unread messages do you have right now — and which app are you most avoiding?
You accidentally send a message to the wrong person. Walk us through your next 30 seconds.
What's something you do on autopilot that you only noticed when someone pointed it out?
Are you someone who's early, exactly on time, or always has a reason for being late?
What's your strategy when someone you don't want to lend money to asks you for it?
What's the one thing your mom has said in front of your friends that made you want to disappear?
What does your ideal do-nothing day actually look like?
Which one are you after a night out — first to fall asleep, last one standing, or the one creating chaos?
What's a topic you know way too much about for no real reason?
What's the fastest you've ever gotten ready to go out — and where were you going that made it worth it?
What do you do when someone is clearly telling a story wrong — and the story is about you?
What's the most Nepali jugaad solution you've pulled off when something broke at home?
When a relative asks 'Ke gardai chau ajhkal?' — what do you actually say vs what you want to say?
You're watching a movie with your parents and an unexpected kissing scene comes on. Describe the next 10 seconds.
What's one café or restaurant in your city you'd recommend to everyone in this room right now?
What's the most Nepali thing you do that you'd struggle to explain to a foreigner?
If you had to survive a 6-hour load shedding with no phone — what would you actually do?
Your dad asks you to explain something on his phone. How many minutes before you lose patience?
You're in a lift with someone you vaguely know. It's 6 floors. What do you do?
What's the one app on your phone you'd be most embarrassed about if someone scrolled through it?
Be honest — are you actually listening when someone talks or are you just waiting for your turn?
What's your move when a waiter gets your order completely wrong but you don't want to make it awkward?
What's the one notification sound that immediately stresses you out?
You show up to plans and the vibe is completely not what you expected. Do you stay and adjust or start planning your exit?
What's the most 'I deserve this' purchase you've made recently?
You're introduced to someone and immediately forget their name. What do you do for the rest of the conversation?
Someone hands you something homemade to try and it's not good. What do you do?
Someone confidently mispronounces something the entire conversation. Do you correct them or let it go?
You're at a wedding and you don't know anyone. What's your opening move?
What's a Nepali tradition you respect but wouldn't personally carry forward?
What's something the older generation in Nepal gets completely right that your generation refuses to admit?
What's a 'success' that Nepali society celebrates that you personally find hollow?
What's your biggest time-wasting habit and have you actually tried to fix it?
Are you someone who makes decisions quickly and moves on, or someone who replays them for weeks?
What's your honest approach when a friend is going through something hard — fixer, listener, or distractor?
What's something you wish people understood about you without you having to explain it every time?
Are you the person your friends call at 2am — and do you actually want to be?
What's your actual relationship with money — spender, saver, or someone who avoids thinking about it entirely?
What's something about Nepal that makes you angry but also makes you stay?
Do you see yourself in Nepal five years from now — and how do you feel about that answer?
What's the most out-of-character thing you've ever done — and what pushed you to do it?
What's something small that genuinely makes your day better every time without fail?
What's something a stranger did or said that stuck with you longer than it should have?
What's something in someone else that you're secretly a little jealous of?
What's a habit your parents have that you swore you'd never do — but now catch yourself doing?
Be honest — when was the last time you cleaned your room and what finally pushed you to do it?
What's something you do every Dashain that you secretly find exhausting but would never admit?
What's something your parents brag about you to relatives that makes you cringe every time?
What's the one auntie or uncle comment that has lived rent-free in your head?
What's one thing you've done in the last year that your younger self would be surprised by?
What's the most uncomfortable conversation you've overheard?
Be honest — when did you last do something purely for yourself with zero guilt?
Be honest — are you the same person at home as you are in front of people?
What's the most honest thing someone has ever said to your face?
What's one thing about your city that you'd fix first if you actually had the power to?
What's something about money you wish someone had told you earlier?
Have you ever pretended to agree with someone just to avoid a longer conversation?
When someone asks how you are, how often do you actually tell the truth?
What's a habit you have that people who know you well would immediately recognize as yours?
What's a compliment you've received that you still don't fully believe about yourself?
Have you ever ended a friendship? What happened?
What emotion do you find hardest to express?
When's the angriest you've ever been at someone you love?
What's something you've never admitted to your parents that would genuinely change how they see you?
Have you ever liked someone your family would never approve of and what did you do about it?
What's the most honest thing you've ever said to someone that changed everything between you?
What's something you've done that completely contradicts the image people have of you?
What's the boldest thing you've ever done for someone you liked?
What's a boundary you've set that people in your life still don't fully respect?
What's the most uncomfortable truth you've had to tell someone you care about?
What's the one thing standing between you and the life you actually want?
What's the most unexpected thing that has made you genuinely happy recently?
What's the most spontaneous decision you've ever made and how did it turn out?
Have you ever surprised yourself by how strongly you felt about something or someone?
What's a moment where someone completely restored your faith in people?
What's something you've held onto for too long — an object, a feeling, or a person?
What's a moment you wish you could relive — not to change it, just to feel it again?
What's a relationship in your life that looks small from the outside but means everything to you?
Have you ever felt like you were exactly where you were supposed to be — when and where was it?
What's something you do that you know is irrational but brings you genuine comfort?
What's a side of you that comes out only around certain people?
What's the most grown-up decision you've ever made?
What's something you've wanted to say to someone in this room since the game started?
What's something you've said to someone that you hope they still remember?

actions.text: Everyone writes down one genuine compliment for the person on their left and reads it out loud.
Everyone has to say one thing they genuinely admire about the person who just answered the last question.
Each person shares a unique skill they have that nobody in the room knows about — the group votes on whose is most surprising.
Each person draws a self-portrait in 30 seconds and the group guesses whose is whose.
Everyone votes on who in the group would be the best politician in Nepal — and who would be the worst. Explain both.
Go around the group — each person has to say one assumption they made about someone in the room when they first met them tonight.
Everyone closes their eyes. The group has to guess without looking — who in the room laughs the loudest? Who goes quiet when they're uncomfortable? Everyone reveals their answer at the same time.
Everyone stands up and swaps seats based on who the group thinks has the most similar energy to each other.
The group takes a vote — who had the most surprising answer tonight? That person gets to skip the next question card if they want.
Everyone simultaneously points to the person in the room they'd most want on their side in an argument.
The group agrees to come back and play Ganthan again within a month — but this time each person has to bring someone new. Agree on the date before this card goes down.
Each person commits to doing one kind thing for a stranger this week. Report back in 7 days.
The group adopts one rule to live by for the next week — agree on what it is before this card goes down. Report back in 7 days.
Everyone in the group has to try something the person on their left recommended tonight — report back in the group chat within a week.
The group plans one meal together at a restaurant — decide the place and date before this card goes down. One person is in charge of making the reservation.
